content.full_text:
Assembly
California Legislature

ROBERT C. FRAZEE
MEMBER OF THE ASSEMBLY
SEVENTY FOURTH DISTRICT

SENT VIA TELEFAX

March 1, 1991

John Wallace, Legal Council
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 94102-5377

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I am writing as a follow-up to our conversation today regarding specific limitations or exclusions (under Proposition 112) which pertain to designated employees of the California Assembly.

Based on our conversation, it is my understanding that travel, food and lodging expenses, which are paid by a governmental agency and related to official government business, must be disclosed on the 730 Form (Conflict of Interest) but are not subject to the $250.00 gift limitation.

I would appreciate your confirmation of this point and any additional legal advice you can provide concerning the applicability of Government Code Sections 89506 and 89513.

Thank you for your assistance with this matter.

Sincerely,

LEE LA FAVE
Field Representative
